name: Termy Rzymskie (Palac Saturna)
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00-22:00, Fri-Sun 10:00-23:00; Sauna Nights extend to 02:00
price: ~80-120 PLN day pass (sauna zone); overnight from 320 PLN incl. baths
highlights: Home to the Colosseum Sauna Arena — the world's largest sauna seating 350 people, with theatrical Aufguss performances. The complex spans 8,000 m² with 11 dry saunas, 5 steam baths, an outdoor Russian banya, infrared cabins, and 5 pools. Regular Sauna Nights are a highlight of the Polish sauna calendar.

name: Aquapark Wroclaw Saunarium
highlights: The largest saunarium in Poland with 20 saunas across multiple themed zones — including 10 dry saunas, 3 biosaunas, 2 steam saunas, a stone sauna, salt sauna, ice cave, and infrared cabins. The outdoor sauna village adds a unique open-air experience. Regular Aufguss sessions with trained sauna masters make this a top-tier Polish sauna destination.

name: SaunaPark Platinium
highlights: Krakow's largest saunarium with 10 different saunas, two pools, a double jacuzzi, and relaxation zones. Known for its atmospheric Sauna Nights (20:00-01:00) which celebrate sauna culture with themed ceremonies, music, and rituals. One of Poland's newer dedicated sauna facilities with strong community following.

name: Sauna Wisla
hours: Open 24h; free sessions on select mornings/afternoons (Wed-Sun)
price: Varies by time slot; some sessions free
highlights: A charming riverside sauna on the Vistula in Warsaw's Czerniakowski Port basin. The wood-fired Finnish sauna reaches 95°C and holds up to 8 people, creating an intimate experience. Features a cold plunge pool and the unique ability to cool off with Vistula river views. Some sessions are completely free, making it one of Warsaw's most accessible authentic sauna experiences.

name: Studio Sante Uzdrowisko Miejskie
highlights: Warsaw's premier modern wellness complex featuring multiple saunas, a hydrogen SPA, collagen light therapy, brine graduation tower, swimming pool, jacuzzi, tepidarium, and Goldarium. Sauna sessions with professional sauna masters are held regularly. A sleek urban retreat combining traditional sauna culture with cutting-edge wellness technology.

name: Wodny Park Warszawianka
highlights: One of the few places in Poland with an authentic Russian Banya alongside Finnish and Turkish saunas. The Finnish sauna reaches 107°C. Features daily evening sauna rituals with a sauna master, a caldarium with multimedia effects, an ice well, snow cave, and a gentle biosauna with essential oils. The Banya steam experience is distinctive and hard to find elsewhere in Poland.

name: AB Wellness Premium Sauna & Spa
highlights: An intimate Warsaw spa complex offering a Finnish sauna, Russian steam room (banya), hammam, jacuzzi, and a terrace with cold plunge pool and open fire. The combination of three bathing traditions — Finnish, Russian, and Turkish — in one small venue makes it unique. A more boutique, atmospheric alternative to Warsaw's larger sauna facilities.

name: Chocholowskie Termy Saunarium
price: 85 PLN (3h), 115 PLN (4h); high season ~10-30 PLN more
highlights: Poland's largest thermal complex at the foot of the Tatra Mountains, with over 40 pools and a dedicated saunarium offering Finnish sauna, juniper BIO sauna, highland cottage sauna, infrared, salt grotto, and salt chamber with water cascade. Open Sauna Nights are held regularly. The mountain backdrop with Tatra views from the outdoor areas is spectacular.

name: Terma Bania
highlights: A Tatra Mountain thermal complex with a dedicated adult-only sauna zone featuring Finnish sauna, steam baths, salt cave, saline pool, and jacuzzi. Daily sauna ceremonies run throughout the day, including a special ladies-only session and an evening smoke ritual with menthol crystals. The mountain setting near Zakopane makes it a natural post-ski sauna destination.

name: Termy Gorący Potok — Gawra Sauna
price: ~60-80 PLN (saunarium zone, separate from pool entry)
highlights: Home to the Gawra — the first large wood-fired sauna with a multimedia screen in Poland, purpose-built for sauna ceremonies. Poland's only natural geothermal thermal baths, combined with an authentic wood-burning sauna experience. Also features aromatic dry and steam saunas, Podhale mud vats, salt graduation tower, and three atmospheric pools fed by natural hot springs.

name: Termy Bukovina Saunarium
price: 29-39 PLN per sauna entry (on top of pool ticket)
highlights: One of Poland's largest sauna centers in scenic Bukowina Tatrzanska, offering 8 sauna types: Finnish, steam, infrared, laconium, and more, with dedicated relaxation and cooling zones. The panoramic views of the Tatra Mountains from the thermal pools and sauna terrace are among the best in Poland. A strong pick for combining mountain holidays with quality sauna time.

name: Aqua Park Zakopane Saunarium
hours: High season: daily 12:00-22:00; Low season: Mon-Fri 14:00-22:00, Sat-Sun 12:00-22:00
highlights: A mountain sauna in Poland's most famous winter resort town. The non-textile saunarium features Finnish sauna, salt water sauna, bio sauna, steam bath, and cold cabin. While not the most elaborate sauna setup, the Zakopane location makes it a convenient choice after a day of hiking or skiing in the Tatras.

name: Termy Maltanskie — Swiat Saun
highlights: Poznan's flagship sauna complex within the Malta Thermal Baths — the largest geothermal water sports complex in Poland. The Swiat Saun (Sauna World) spans 1,000 m² with 14 rooms including indoor and outdoor saunas, dry and wet options, and guided aromatherapy sessions. Highly rated for the variety and quality of sauna experiences at reasonable prices.

name: Wanga Sauna & Spa
price: ~80-120 PLN single entry; multi-passes up to 40% cheaper
highlights: Pomerania's newest and most ambitious saunarium near the Tricity. Features an event sauna for 150 people (ideal for spectacular group ceremonies), alongside Finnish, infrared, salt, herbal, and steam saunas plus a Russian banya. The outdoor jacuzzi and cold water pool sit among trees. Quickly becoming a hub for the Tricity sauna community with regular themed events.

name: Limitless by Autopay — Beach Saunas
highlights: A unique beachside sauna experience just 80 meters from the Baltic Sea, set in the coastal forest of Sopot. Dry saunas, ice baths, and fitness equipment in a stunning natural setting. One of very few places in Poland where you can go from sauna directly to a Baltic Sea plunge. Also hosts occasional sauna rave events combining heat, music, and community.

name: Suntago Park of Poland — Saunaria
highlights: Europe's largest indoor water park includes a dedicated Saunaria zone with 15 themed saunas. The Egyptian Village features five themed saunas (Sahara, Ramzesa, Cheopsa, Sfinksa, Kamienna Krypta), plus Finnish saunas, bio saunas, infrared, a forest inhalation sauna, and classic steam. Sauna ceremonies with music and aromatherapy run throughout the day. Grand in scale but heavy on theming.

name: Fabryka Wody Saunarium
price: 50 PLN/h normal, 40 PLN/h discount; 179 PLN/month pass
highlights: Northern Poland's largest saunarium spanning 2,500 m² across 3 levels with 16 themed saunas. Creative designs include a Hawaiian sauna, a maritime sauna with boat-shaped seating, a volcanic sauna with visual eruption effects, hammam, and child-friendly saunas. Opened in 2024, it brings a modern and ambitious sauna culture to Szczecin on the Odra River.

name: Termy Uniejow
highlights: A unique thermal complex in central Poland near a 14th-century castle, using natural geothermal waters. Features year-round outdoor brine pools, recreational pools, jacuzzis, and an indoor Wellness & SPA zone with dry and steam saunas plus an ice chamber. The historic setting beside Uniejow Castle distinguishes it from Poland's mountain thermal resorts. A strong day-trip option from Lodz.

name: Termy Warminskie
highlights: A natural hot-spring thermal complex in Poland's Warmia-Masuria lake district, far from the crowded Tatra Mountain resorts. Features 7 indoor pools, an outdoor pool, and a dry sauna zone all fed by natural geothermal springs. The northern Poland location near the Masurian Lakes makes it a distinctive choice for combining lake holidays with authentic thermal bathing in a quieter setting.
